item.description: The pain on Zelenskyy's face says it all, photo taken whilst he visited the site of the atrocities in Bucha. Donkey face LAVROV denies Bucha and blames USA

item.title: Russian draft in Luhansk
item.description: A bunch of Russians going door-to-door in Luhansk issuing draft notices. They ask where they lady’s son is. She says that the he had left and he was not at home. The soldier then asked "why are you filming?" The lady replies: “for a trial in The Hague".

item.title: Gotta have a good Monday to have a good week!
item.description:
Monday, funday. Try and find something more fun to focus on than the start of the workweek. As always none of it is OC, and it's all meant in jest.
2

49
50 Can't believe what a struggle it was to edit this one.... Anyway be good to each other! Woop woop, personal best! Feel free to say hey and ask questions! MV Edit. Always fun to make most viral. For those that are interested, here's links to other similar stuff. 29: https://imgur.com/gallery/K5zEezV 28: https://imgur.com/gallery/qhifSb1 27: https://imgur.com/gallery/rWDJlZl 26: https://imgur.com/gallery/vui62Hf 25: https://imgur.com/gallery/6Xa9v4t 24: https://imgur.com/gallery/NPkZGWk 23: https://imgur.com/gallery/VGkwrdS 22: https://imgur.com/gallery/OsoKl7W 21: https://imgur.com/gallery/EXWbc1u 20 :https://imgur.com/gallery/5FvEFdm 19: https://imgur.com/gallery/q6juBR9 18: https://imgur.com/gallery/WXhygYp 17: https://imgur.com/gallery/KCVrSCL 16: https://imgur.com/gallery/6NrSsyV 16: https://imgur.com/gallery/1MKS6dK 14: https://imgur.com/gallery/g5oOjmB 13: https://imgur.com/gallery/6HguL7m 12: https://imgur.com/gallery/gXstg2w 10: https://imgur.com/gallery/H0EqBWM 9: https://imgur.com/gallery/8I2Q7l8 8: http://imgur.com/gallery/OHgyf1T 7: https://imgur.com/gallery/Rmvffx3 6: https://imgur.com/gallery/S1SxD7Z 5: https://imgur.com/gallery/ltPgUhK 4: https://imgur.com/gallery/uNLAR4T 3: https://imgur.com/gallery/tKhLPCt 2: https://imgur.com/gallery/0HmYMTH 1: https://imgur.com/gallery/RLfsBYw Spoiler: they are all mature Misc edit. A LOT of people are commenting on Hooters, which is not what I expected. In short though. Fuck Hooters seems to be the common comment message (and one which I agree with). Nuke the franchise and let one actually focused on the food take its place.

item.title: At least I told them
item.description: In a long term marriage. Talked with my wife about everything and we both come to realize I'm gay. She was 100% on the same page, supporting me and agreed to stay close friends. Told my parents and their response was how they were heartbroken, don't believe it's true and couldn't believe how I had chosen to destroy my marriage. My mom could not understand that my wife was not totally devastated and didn't hate me. Family basically made it all about them and how they were so hurt by my "choice". I expected they wouldn't approve, but it hurts way more than I thought it would - especially that they didn't ever consider or ask how I was doing. I know I'm going to be OK, but it sucks a lot. Long Edit for clarity/context: My wife and I have been together for 16 years and have a kid together. I was raised religious and was very much anti-gay for a long time. Eventually, I realized how much hate it created in me and how much pain it caused others to live that way and I had to change. I never truly considered that I might be gay because by the time I was really no longer anti-gay, I was already married. I had come a long way by that point, but still had a very long way to go. When I really let myself explore the idea, I realized I was probably bi. Had that conversation with my wife and as we did, we both were like, actually, maybe I'm more than just bi. Explored it with her permission with a guy and it was like a switch got flipped and I was like 'oh, apparently I'm very gay'. My parents aren't terrible people, but they do, like everyone does, have blind spots. I don't hate them, and I do believe they've grown a lot since I was a kid, but they still got more growing to do. We've always been pretty close, but over the last few years, we've drifted apart as I've become far more liberal and they've become far more conservative. I've also had a ton of support from friends, co-workers and strangers on the internet. Which has been amazing. I just wish I could get support from the family I really care about. I'm doing better, but it still hurts a lot and I don't know if I'll ever be OK with them after this - even if they do accept me eventually.
item.dc:creator: wontondisregard

item.title: Its nice to have a president who doesnt call putin and ask him if he did war crimes, then say "I dont see why he would" and let it go.

item.title: What leadership looks like
item.description:
Seeing how Zelenskyy has treated his nation has really underscored for me, the difference between politicians and leaders.
These photos are of his visit to Bucha. Edit: Photo by Metin Aktas,
Photo by Metin Aktas
Photo by Metin Aktas
Photo by Metin Aktas
Photo by RONALDO SCHEMIDT
He’s not playing a tough guy. Photo by RONALDO SCHEMIDT
Edit: This image is from Kyiv in late February early March. It is not from the recent visit to Bucha.
He’s not tossing paper towels in performative service, or wearing a ball cap to look relatable. Photo provided by Presidential Press Service
Photo provided by Presidential Press Service
Photo provided by Presidential Press Service
He’s connecting with people where they are.
item.dc:creator: Mbcb350

item.title: Canceled: This week's cocaine sex orgy
item.dc:creator: jfoobar

item.title: Walmart has gotten away with so much and no one cares because they dont have a Bezos to meme about.
item.description: I work at Walmart and have for a few years now. Once Covid happened Walmart immediatly got a lot worse to work with. And I should remind, they are a much bigger employer than Amazon. They added an Online Grocery department but barely touched payroll. Result? Most departments had serious cuts to personel so we're at a point now where we are required to do twice as much work as we used to. Multiple workers have been experiencing new mental and physical conditions due to the new demands on them and constant negative pressure from management who arent permitted to admit anything is wrong. My entire body always hurts, I'm always tired, and I havent had a break in months. They said they were starting covid guidelines, but never enforced them. So workers have died due to walmarts refusal to actually do anything. They removed many middle tier positions leaving far less mobility while shoveling more tasks on to those that remain while decreasing pay in those positions. They removed evaluations which decreased annual raises. Severely. They removed bonuses, but then added them back at a major reduction. Then tied them to store evaluations which are always failed now due to also removing normal checkout lines in favor of self check. And if that wasnt enough, they then turned around and tied the bonus to employee training, which is never done due to afformentioned cuts to payroll. They removed set schedules, so you only get two days off in a row if the boss likes you. They cut time off gain. They made it far easier for a manager to fire you by changing the coaching system to three strikes for anything and you're out. When before it was three strikes for the same thing. In other words, just giving management free reign to fire anyone. Above are things instituted in the last two years. Below is just general BS: They have almost zero oversight over managers who are permitted to do as they please. Ethics ALWAYS sides with management. My own manager threatens people constantly, talks bad about employees and the store as a whole constantly, demands we answer personal questions, hires on her friends, openly admits to firing people she doesn't like and so on. Ethics has not done a single thing despite a massive amount of people filing the same complaints. Still the only company I've ever heard of with no time and a half on holidays. They make us take off christmas and thanksgiving, but also FORCE us to use our severely limited time off for it. Ethics will sometimes just plain ignore you. I was given a ethics "contact" which was a disconnected number. When I called back about it was told to "just keep trying". They will close stores for Union talk. I really do feel for the Amazon workers and the horrible things Amazon does. But it pains me that no one seems to give a shit about other companies. What, is Walmart to much of a 90's thing? Is it too trashy? EDIT: Thank you for the kind words. But "Just quit" isnt an option. I am actively looking for other work, there is just very little. "Just unionize" isnt an option due to the size of the beast and its willingness to kill off entire stores to end that talk. I hate working here but I have to, even if it often has me considering ending myself. EDIT2: I've been advised that they actually still have no bonuses for normal associates, only supervisors and up. #walmart #workers_rights #hell #waltons
item.dc:creator: Ikusame
